summary: The diff adds a Motoko single-actor backend skeleton plus a React/React-Query frontend with Internet Identity login UI, profile setup, basic job browsing cards, and an apply-to-job dialog. Several core requested flows (provider-only job lifecycle, applicant review, local-match highlighting, and stable upgrade persistence) are not evidenced in the provided diff summary. The diff also introduces some unrequested elements (e.g., caffeine.ai footer promotion, admin-token based access-control initialization, and extra backend fields/types).

missing_requirements.requirement: Job posting functionality for Providers: create, view, edit, close/reopen, and delete job listings; each job includes title, description, area, category/tags, and status; job list supports filtering by area and category/tags.
missing_requirements.reason: The frontend shows a CreateJob dialog and a JobCard and a jobSearch hook, but the diff summary does not show any UI or backend endpoints for edit, close/reopen, or delete; also tags (plural) are not evidenced (only a single `category : Text`). Filtering UI controls are not shown, only a search hook.

missing_requirements.requirement: Area-based matching flow: workers apply; providers review applicants and accept/reject; system highlights 'local' matches when worker area and job area match (exact or simple normalized).
missing_requirements.reason: Worker apply is evidenced via ApplyToJobDialog and useApplyToJob, but no applicant review UI (per-job applicant list) is shown, and no 'local match' highlighting logic/UI is evidenced. The application status mutation hook exists, but the provider-facing review screen and the backend matching/normalization rules are not shown in the truncated backend.

missing_requirements.requirement: Persist all core data across canister upgrades using stable storage patterns; expose typed API for CRUD and queries; enforce authorization checks based on caller Principal and profile role.
missing_requirements.reason: The backend uses in-memory `Map.empty` for profiles/jobs/applications with no visible stable storage or upgrade hooks in the provided excerpt. Also, role enforcement for provider-only actions cannot be confirmed from the truncated `createJob` implementation (it checks `#user` permission, not explicitly provider role, in the visible portion).

missing_requirements.requirement: Build main frontend screens including provider applicant review and worker application status; all API interactions use React Query with loading/error states and cache invalidation after mutations.
missing_requirements.reason: Routes/pages are referenced (BrowseJobsPage, ProviderJobsPage, MyApplicationsPage, JobDetailPage, etc.), but their implementations are not shown in the diff summary, so key screens (notably provider applicant review and worker application status UI) and consistent React Query loading/error handling across all interactions cannot be verified from the provided evidence.

hallucinated_features.description: Footer includes a promotional outbound link to caffeine.ai ('Built with ... using caffeine.ai').
hallucinated_features.reason: Not requested by any requirement; introduces third-party branding/linking in user-facing UI.

hallucinated_features.description: Frontend initializes backend access control using a secret URL parameter `caffeineAdminToken` via `_initializeAccessControlWithSecret`.
hallucinated_features.reason: BuildRequest specifies only Internet Identity for auth and does not request an admin-token/secret-parameter mechanism; this is additional authorization behavior not described.

hallucinated_features.description: Backend job type includes an optional `salary : ?Nat` field and backend defines a `Matches` type with a `score` for matching.
hallucinated_features.reason: BuildRequest does not request salary support or scoring-based matching; it explicitly calls for simple area-based highlighting rather than scoring/ranking.
